title: Eriostemon dentatus
taxonomy: Eriostemon dentatus is classified within the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the citrus or rue family. This family comprises a diverse group of flowering plants, including citrus trees, rue, and various species of Eriostemon. The genus Eriostemon is native to Australia and consists of several species of shrubs and small trees. Eriostemon dentatus is a species of flowering plant endemic to New South Wales, Australia. It is a member of the Rutaceae family, known for its fragrant flowers and leathery leaves.

intro: Eriostemon dentatus, also known as the pink feather flower, is a small shrub native to Australia, characterized by its fragrant pink flowers and grey-green foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in relieving digestive issues and sore throats. Bioactive constituents of Eriostemon dentatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
